channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Feeling lonely? Twitter cofounder Ev Williams has an app for that
item.description: When Twitter cofounder and Medium founder Evan “Ev” Williams was planning his 50th birthday party, he didn’t know who to invite. Having spent more of his life building and scaling tech companies, he found himself feeling disconnected from friends.
item.dc:creator: Yasmin Gagne, Fast Company

item.title: You freak out when battery life hits 38%, but here's how to extend it and calm the heck down
item.description: A new report puts our smartphone battery concerns in perspective and we have some tips to make it through the day.
item.dc:creator: lance.ulanoff@futurenet.com (Lance Ulanoff), TechRadar

item.title: Senate Democrat presses Noem on Real ID deadline
item.description: Sen. Jack Reed (D-R.I.) pressed Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on the Real ID requirements being implemented in two weeks. The new measure requires individuals to provide a U.S. birth certificate or a U.S. passport, or a permanent resident card (green card) if they are a non-U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump administration replaces federal COVID-19 response page with 'lab leak' explainer
item.description: The Trump White House on Friday replaced the covid.gov website that provided resources and information on COVID-19 with a webpage promoting the theory that the pandemic was a result of a laboratory leak in Wuhan, China, which a House GOP panel strongly favored in a report released last year.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: The US has a single rare earths mine. Chinese export limits are energizing a push for more
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — America’s only rare earths mine heard from anxious companies soon after China responded to President Donald Trump’s tariffs this month by limiting exports of those minerals used for military applications and in many high-tech devices.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Everything new on Hulu in May 2025 – stream my favorite Pamela Anderson movie, celebrate Star Wars Day with the original trilogy and more
item.description: From reality TV and the original Star Wars trilogy, here's the full list of titles coming to Hulu in May 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Instagram launches ‘Blend’ to share personalized Reels with friends
item.description: When it comes to sharing Instagram Reels with friends, the process of three taps to get a Reel from A to B can feel surprisingly tedious. Now, Instagram has addressed that issue with its latest feature: Instagram Blend.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida AG Issues Subpoena to Roblox Over Child Safety Concerns
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier demands information from Roblox regarding marketing, age verification, and moderation policies amid concerns over child safety.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: CIOs must mind their own data confidence gap
item.description: As far as many C-suite business and IT executives are concerned, their company data is in great shape, capable of fueling data-driven decision-making and delivering AI-powered solutions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Nvidia announces plans to make AI supercomputers in US
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Spotify down for thousands of users
item.description: Music streaming service Spotify was down for hours on Wednesday for tens of thousands of users. The outage began around Wednesday morning, with more than 48,600 users reporting issues around the same time at 9:40 a.m. EDT, according to outage tracking site Downdetector.com.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JRuby 10 brings faster startup times
item.description: JRuby 10, the latest release of the Ruby language variant built atop the JVM, has arrived, bringing startup time improvements, support for Java 21, and compatibility with Ruby 3.4. Release of JRuby 10 was announced April 14. JRuby 10 can be downloaded from jruby.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Measuring success in dataops, data governance, and data security
item.description: Back in 2006, British mathematician Clive Humby stated that data was the new oil. Like oil, data isn’t useful in its raw state and must be refined, processed, and distributed to deliver value.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI isn’t one size fits all as federal agencies seek the right AI tools for mission success
item.description: Federal agencies should consider widening their focus on AI implementations beyond just generative AI. The post AI isn’t one size fits all as federal agencies seek the right AI tools for mission success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: CrowdStrike’s field tech strategist explains how AWS Control Tower plus CrowdStrike Falcon lets new workloads inherit access controls in zero trust environments The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: CrowdStrike’s Dennen Monks on ensuring none of your assets escape zero trust coverage first
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: White House record-keeping revamp
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
